backend: ngsolve (proof of concept; pattern applies to skfem / kratos / fenics / dune / fourc)
surface_symptom: Senior-AI-scientist critic round 4 verdict + own empirical audit found that 6 of 7 backends had ZERO pitfalls in Table-1 ([Category] + Signal:) format (deal.II was the only promoted backend). The 13 non-deal.II Tier-2 fixtures used SYNTHETIC pitfall_index values (e.g., 92, 93, 95, 96, 97, 88, 89) — most of which exceeded the actual pitfall count of their target physics (NGSolve maxwell had 7 pitfalls, fixtures referenced index 93/95). So the Tier-2 work for non-deal.II backends was building a parallel set of operationally-verified behaviors that the MCP's catalog retrieval (`prepare_simulation`) could not surface to the agent: a user asking the MCP for Maxwell-on-NGSolve pitfalls got 7 descriptive prose bullets that the post-execution critic could not grep against any captured output. The empirical NGSolve probe confirmed the catalog drift hypothesis the critic predicted — the 30% catalog-bug rate from deal.II's cheap-bucket audit was almost certainly higher for unaudited backends.
root_cause: PR #26 (the Table-1 discipline rollout) promoted only the deal.II catalog. The cross-backend pitfall format gap was tracked as 'task #27 NGSolve failing templates' etc., but in practice the focus stayed on deal.II depth (96 pitfalls, ~50% Tier-2 by end of Q2) while skfem/NGSolve/Kratos/FEniCSx/DUNE/4C catalogs accumulated 756 prose-format pitfalls that no critic gate could verify against output. Verify_signal_clauses.py compounded this by only carrying a deal.II-specific code_symbol set: ELEMENT_NAMES + 89 hand-curated deal.II symbols, plus a generic Krylov/MPI list. NGSolve / skfem / Kratos symbols like HCurl, BilinearForm, NgException, ModelPart, Variable were not in the entity set, so even hand-promoted NGSolve pitfalls would have failed Tier-0 by 100%.
pitfall_db_entries: [Process] Cross-backend Tier-0 verification requires a per-backend code_symbol entity set. Without it, Table-1 promotion of one backend cannot be validated by the same falsifiability metric; mixed-format catalogs hide regressions. Mitigation in this commit: _load_entity_split now branches on backend ∈ {ngsolve, skfem, kratos, fenics, dune, fourc} and adds ~30-100 backend-specific code symbols each. Future Tier-0 measurements are per-backend honest.

[Integration] Fixture pitfall_index synchronicity check is missing — the runner accepts any integer regardless of whether the target catalog has that many pitfalls. Add a Layer A→B test that flags fixture.json files whose pitfall_index >= len(catalog[physics].pitfalls). Currently 11 of 13 non-deal.II fixtures fail this implicit invariant (NGSolve, skfem, Kratos, FEniCSx — all but kratos::linear_elasticity::0 and skfem::linear_elasticity::0). Without this check, Tier-2 'passes' do not lift catalog status because the catalog has no corresponding entry.

[Numerical] NGSolve maxwell pitfall promotion 2026-05-31 extends the prose 7-bullet list to 10 Table-1 entries — original 7 reworded with [Category] / Signal: vocabulary, plus 3 new pitfalls that already had operational fixtures (curl on H1 → maxwell#7, grad on HCurl → maxwell#8, vector source on scalar form deferred to poisson promotion). Result: 2 fixtures now catalog-indexed (was 0). Replicate this pattern for each backend × physics combination.
agent_detection_after_fix: verify_signal_clauses.py --backend ngsolve scoreboard moved from 0/120 Table-1 to 10/123 (the maxwell physics specifically). Tier-0+1 went 0 → 8 (8 of 10 maxwell pitfalls cite real NGSolve code symbols like HCurl, BilinearForm, NgException; the remaining 2 reference math/physics concepts and would benefit from a Signal-text tightening pass). Tier-2 catalog-indexed went 0 → 2 (the h1_curl_operator_unknown and hcurl_grad_mismatch fixtures, previously at synthetic indices 93/95, now correctly attach to maxwell#7/#8). Runner-level Tier-2 stays at 26/26. The pattern is reproducible: each non-deal.II backend × physics combo can be promoted with ~30 min of catalog rewriting + entity-set audit. Honest 7-backend scoreboard achievable in ~2 weeks per critic recommendation, vs the 4–8 week deal.II medium-bucket sprint that was deferred.
